page: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor operating at 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 9 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance across multiple domains. The processor's support for advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), contributes to strong scores in Passmark CPU extended instructions and encryption tests. Memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 4,110.43 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 222.41 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database and web serving workloads, the instance achieved 64.03 million Redis SET operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 73.67 GB/sec. LLM inference speeds for prompt processing using a medium 7B model reached a strong 195.39 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i-flex.12xlarge delivers high resource density and strong benchmark-per-resource metrics for general-purpose workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. However, tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage, and a moderate baseline network bandwidth of 9 Gbps. This hardware profile is best suited for workloads such as database operations, web serving, software compilation, and moderate machine learning inference tasks that benefit from Intel AMX and AVX-512 acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It includes 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance demonstrates strong benchmark performance, particularly in memory bandwidth, database operations, and cryptographic execution, aided by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. It delivers 64.03 million Redis SET operations per second and 73.67 GB/sec static web serving throughput. Tradeoffs include a baseline network bandwidth of 9 Gbps and reliance on external storage. This instance is configured for database hosting, web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor, delivering strong database and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, DDR5 memory, and advanced vector and matrix instruction acceleration.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 8488C processor with AMX and AVX-512 instruction support
Strong memory bandwidth and database performance metrics
No local storage and 9 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation
